parameters.content:
## 🎙️ Product Update Audio Announcements — Changelog to TTS Audio

**What this workflow does:**
Every time a new release is pushed to your GitHub repository (or a Notion changelog page is updated), this workflow:
1. Fetches the raw changelog/release notes from GitHub Releases API
2. Pulls the matching structured release notes from a **Notion** database (rich formatted content)
3. Uses **OpenAI** to rewrite the technical changelog into a natural, podcast-style spoken script
4. Sends the script to **ElevenLabs TTS API** to generate a professional MP3 audio file
5. Uploads the MP3 via **UploadToURL** to get a permanent public audio URL
6. Uses the **HTML node** to compose a beautifully formatted email newsletter with the audio player embed, changelog summary, and a 'Listen Now' CTA button
7. Sends the newsletter via **Gmail** to your subscriber list (loaded from Google Sheets)
8. Posts a Slack message to your internal `#product-updates` channel with the audio URL and release summary
9. **Writes back** to the Notion changelog page — marks it as 'Audio Published' with the hosted audio URL

**Architecture (unique from all previous templates):**
- 🐙 GitHub Trigger (watches releases — NEW trigger type)
- 📓 Notion — fetch release notes page (NEW: database query)
- 🤖 OpenAI — rewrite to spoken script (NEW: different prompt purpose)
- 🎙️ ElevenLabs TTS API — audio generation (NEW: TTS node)
- ☁️ UploadToURL — host the MP3 file (mandatory)
- 📋 Aggregate Node — bundle subscriber emails (NEW node type)
- 🌐 HTML Node — compose rich email body (NEW node type)
- 📧 Gmail — send newsletter to subscribers (NEW: email delivery)
- 💬 Slack — post to #product-updates channel (NEW: Slack node)
- 🔄 Notion Update — write-back audio URL to source page (NEW: bidirectional)

**Setup Requirements:**
1. GitHub credentials (personal access token with `repo` read scope)
2. Notion Integration Token — share your Changelog database with the integration
3. Notion Database ID for your changelog (from the database page URL)
4. OpenAI API credentials
5. ElevenLabs API Key + your preferred Voice ID (from elevenlabs.io/voices)
6. UploadToURL endpoint configured in upload node
7. Gmail OAuth2 credentials
8. Google Sheets with subscriber emails — sheet named `Subscribers` with column `Email`
9. Slack Bot Token with `chat:write` scope — set channel to `#product-updates`
10. Notion credentials again for the write-back step

nodes.name: 📝 Note — GitHub Trigger, Parse & Notion Fetch

parameters.content:
### 🐙 Step 1 — GitHub Release Trigger & Notion Fetch
**GitHub Trigger:** Fires on every new GitHub Release published to your repo (uses GitHub webhooks — not polling). Captures tag name, release name, release body (raw markdown changelog), author, and release URL.
**Code — Parse Release:** Cleans the raw markdown changelog into plain text. Extracts version number, release date, and categorises changes into `features`, `fixes`, and `improvements` arrays by scanning bullet prefixes.
**Notion — Query Database:** Searches your Notion Changelog database for a page matching the version tag. Retrieves the richer formatted description, product area tags, and any manually added context — supplements the raw GitHub release body.

### 🎙️ Step 2 — Script Writing, TTS Generation & Upload
**OpenAI — Spoken Script:** Rewrites the combined GitHub + Notion content into a warm, conversational podcast-style spoken announcement (60-90 seconds when read aloud). Avoids markdown, bullet points, and jargon.
**ElevenLabs TTS:** Sends the script to ElevenLabs `/v1/text-to-speech/{voice_id}` endpoint. Returns raw MP3 binary audio. Voice, stability, and similarity boost are all configurable.
**UploadToURL:** Uploads the MP3 binary and returns a permanent public URL — this URL is used in the email embed, Slack message, and Notion write-back.

nodes.name: 📝 Note — Subscribers, HTML Build & Gmail Send

parameters.content:
### 📧 Step 3 — Subscriber Load, HTML Email & Gmail Send
**Google Sheets — Load Subscribers:** Reads all rows from the `Subscribers` sheet. Each row contains at minimum an `Email` field. Returns one item per subscriber.
**Aggregate Node:** Bundles all subscriber email addresses into a single comma-separated string — used as the Gmail BCC list so one send covers all subscribers.
**HTML Node:** Composes a fully formatted HTML email with: audio player embed (using `<audio>` tag), release version header, changelog summary table (features/fixes), and a styled 'Listen Now' CTA button linking to the hosted MP3.
**Gmail — Send Newsletter:** Sends the HTML email to all subscribers in BCC. Subject includes version number and release date.

### 💬 Step 4 — Slack Announcement & Notion Write-Back
**Slack — Post to #product-updates:** Posts a rich Slack Block Kit message to your internal channel. Includes: release version, one-line summary, 'Listen to audio update' link (hosted MP3), and a direct link to the GitHub release.
**Notion — Update Page:** Writes back to the original Notion changelog page — sets a `AudioStatus` property to `Published`, stores the `AudioURL` (hosted MP3 link), and stamps the `AudioPublishedAt` timestamp. This prevents duplicate processing if the workflow fires again.

nodes.name: Code — Merge Notion Context + Release Data
Code — Store Script Text Before Upload
Code — Store Audio URL + Full Release Data

parameters.jsCode:
const aggregated = $input.first().json.subscribers || []; const releaseData = $('Code — Store Audio URL + Full Release Data').item.json; // Extract emails into BCC list const emails = aggregated .map(row => row.Email || row.email || '') .filter(Boolean) .join(','); // Build features/fixes HTML rows const featureRows = (releaseData.features || []).map(f => `✨ ${f}` ).join(''); const fixRows = (releaseData.fixes || []).map(f => `🐛 ${f}` ).join(''); const improvRows = (releaseData.improvements || []).map(i => `⚡ ${i}` ).join(''); const changeTable = (featureRows || fixRows || improvRows) ? `${featureRows}${fixRows}${improvRows}
` : `

${releaseData.plainChangelog.substring(0,300)}...

`; const htmlBody = `

🎙️ Product Update

${releaseData.releaseName} · ${releaseData.releaseDate}

🎧 Listen to this update



▶ Listen Now (MP3)

What's new in ${releaseData.version}

${changeTable}
View Full Release Notes on GitHub →

You're receiving this because you subscribed to ${releaseData.repoName} product updates.

`; return [{ json: { ...releaseData, htmlBody, bccList: emails, emailSubject: `🎙️ [${releaseData.version}] ${releaseData.releaseName} — Listen to the Update` } }];

Notion — Write Back Audio URL to Changelog Page
